term: CAESP (Crypto Asset Exchange Service Provider / 暗号資産交換業者)
plain_definition: Japan's licence category for a crypto-asset exchange -- broadly the equivalent of Hong Kong's VATP licence. A firm that buys or sells crypto-assets, exchanges one crypto-asset for another, brokers either activity, or manages crypto-assets on behalf of users, on a business basis, must first register with the Prime Minister (in practice, the Financial Services Agency). Only a registered CAESP -- or a comparably licensed foreign crypto-asset exchange operator -- may lawfully conduct crypto-asset exchange business in Japan.
formal_definition: Payment Services Act (資金決済に関する法律, Act No. 59 of 2009), Chapter 3-3 (第三章の三 暗号資産), inserted by Act No. 62 of 2016 (implementing Cabinet Office Ordinances finalised 24 March 2017; regime effective 1 April 2017). Art. 2(15) defines 'crypto-asset exchange business' (暗号資産交換業) as conducting, on a business basis, any of: buying/selling crypto-assets or exchanging them for other crypto-assets; brokering, intermediating, or acting as agent for that activity; managing users' money in connection with either; or managing crypto-assets for another person. Art. 2(16) defines 'crypto-asset exchange service provider' (暗号資産交換業者) as a person registered under Art. 63-2, which itself prohibits conducting crypto-asset exchange business without that registration. Custody-only business was brought within the registration perimeter, and crypto-asset derivatives dealing moved out of the PSA into FIEA Type I business, by Act No. 28 of 2019, effective 1 May 2020. All confirmed by direct fetch of the current consolidated PSA text via the e-Gov Law API this session (law_id 421AC0000000059).
